description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a visually striking, textured appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark, almost black coloration, while the grooved top surface has a slightly lighter, stone-like gray tone. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from a solid stone material such as granite or basalt. Its proportions suggest it is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly for traffic control or as a protective element. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional components.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, dark sides. The block has a dense, solid appearance and is likely used as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface with the grooves running horizontally and the solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and solid back face, with grooves running horizontally.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or directly on a prepared ground surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
